per_situation.feedback: Highly specific with named regs (Treas. Reg. §1.83-2(b), §1202, $75M gross-assets test), concrete community-property state list, and actionable mailing mechanics. Non-obviousness is strong — spousal signature vulnerability, QSBS asset-threshold cliff across tranches, and the 'clock starts at board-approval date not start date' insight are genuine blind spots. Most factual claims carry [doc-X] citations, though a few specifics (community-property state list, IRS no-extension authority phrasing, specific §83(b) statement contents) ride on adjacent citations. Two distinct community sections present: long-form-references and tax-and-finance-professionals.

Excellent specificity: names IRC §280G with the 3× base amount threshold and 20% excise tax, concrete discount ranges (15-25% public, 30-50% private), specific covenant durations (12 months), and named contract terms (Good Reason, Cause, Change of Control). Non-obviousness is high: 280G parachute, non-compete consideration mechanics, survivor bias on retention attrition, and private-RSU cashflow trap are all genuine blind spots beyond the obvious 'negotiate the trigger.' Grounding is good but not complete — many factual claims (280G mechanics, attrition statistics, RSU blackout drag percentages, non-compete consideration doctrine) carry no citation despite being assertions of fact. Only 2 distinct community sections (matt-levine-school, long-form-references) rather than the 3 domains identified.

per_situation.feedback: Highly specific with named clauses (§422, §83, 83(b), Form 6251, QSBS $75M threshold, $15M exclusion, 26-28% AMT, 22% supplemental withholding, 46% unexercised stat). Surfaces strong non-obvious angles: survivor bias on preference stacks, single-tax-year concentration risk from double-trigger + lockup, joint personal-balance-sheet framing, adverse selection on cash/equity ratio, refresh-grant trajectory. Most factual claims carry [doc-X] citations though some sentences in blind spots and next steps lack markers. Two distinct 'What X would tell you' sections (long-form-references and tax-and-finance-professionals).

Strong response with specific mechanisms (IRC §422, ISO/NSO distinction, 90-day vs 7-10yr PTEP, 4yr/1yr cliff, ~15-25% dilution per round, 0.05-0.25% typical band, AMT preference item). Highly non-obvious framing on meta-signal of percent-vs-dollar quoting, survivor-bias base rates, and year-4 reverse cliff. Two distinct community sections (hn-collective, long-form-references). Many factual claims carry [doc-X] markers but several specific assertions (90-day default, 0.05-0.25% band, ISO tax mechanics details, refresher ~0.25x sizing, ~two-thirds never raise A) appear without consistent citation density.

Highly specific with named IRS sections (1202, 83(b)), concrete deadlines (30-day clock), dollar figures ($15M exclusion, $75M asset threshold, 13.3% CA rate), and named mechanics (5%/2% redemption trap, Rule 1). Non-obviousness is strong: deadlock governance second-order effects, adverse-selection timing framing, vesting-is-symmetric-doesn't-fix-past, CA non-conformity to 1202 — all genuine blind spots beyond the obvious 'negotiate / use vesting' baseline. Grounding is solid: most factual claims carry [doc-1] or [doc-3] markers, though a few claims (CA 13.3% rate specifics, deadlock governance norms, USPS Certified Mail procedure) lack citations. Two distinct community sections present: long-form-references and tax-and-finance-professionals.

Strong specificity with concrete mechanics (Amazon's 5/15/40/40 vest curve, 409A vs preferred, PSU forfeiture, tender windows, garden-leave clauses) and actionable next steps with named clauses to search. Non-obviousness is high: adverse-selection framing of the recruiter pitch, illiquidity differential, years-3-4 refresh gap, and survivor bias are genuinely non-baseline angles. Grounding is solid but imperfect — most factual claims carry [doc-X] markers, but several specific assertions (Amazon's 5/15/40/40 schedule, RSU tax treatment, Meta/Google refresh stacking, ~20% dollar-vested heuristic) are uncited. Diversity is exactly 2 distinct 'What X would tell you' sections (long-form-references and matt-levine-school).

Highly specific with named IRS sections (§1202, §1045, §422), dollar thresholds ($50M/$75M gross assets, $15M exclusion, 10× basis), 60-day rollover window, 180-day lockup, and concrete tax rates (37% ordinary, ~20% LTCG). Non-obviousness is strong: QSBS forfeiture with non-transferability, §1045 rollover, adverse-selection signaling from insider participation, lockup-still-binds-the-rest concentration framing, and pro-rata fill mechanics are all blind spots a typical employee wouldn't surface. Grounding is high — nearly every factual claim carries a [doc-X] marker, though a few sentences in the matt-levine section and the disqualifying-disposition explanation in the long-form section mix uncited assertions with cited ones. Three distinct community perspectives present (long-form-references, matt-levine-school, tax-and-finance-professionals).

Highly specific with concrete numbers (22%/37% supplemental withholding, $1M threshold, 5/15/40/40 Amazon vest, 62% concentration math, 50% counter-offer attrition stat). Surfaces strong non-obvious blind spots: year-5 refresh cliff, counter-offer adverse selection, single-stock correlation between paycheck and equity, supplemental withholding under-collection. Nearly all factual claims carry [doc-1] markers, though heavy reliance on a single doc weakens grounding diversity. Only one 'What [community] would tell you' section present (long-form-references), missing the multi-community structure.

Exceptionally specific: cites WARN Act thresholds (50/500), OWBPA 29 USC §626(f) 21+7 day windows, IRC §422 90-day PTEP, CA Bus & Prof §16600, MA garden-leave 50%, WA salary threshold (~$120k). Strong non-obvious angles: WARN-as-already-owed-consideration framing, OWBPA invalidating the 7-day window, the 44-week unpaid gap math, RSU waiver as cosmetic-vs-substantive, ISO 90-day clock parallel deadline. Grounding is uneven — many factual statutory claims carry [doc-1] markers but the cited doc is an equity guide that explicitly cannot support the employment-law claims (WARN, OWBPA, state non-compete law), so citations are technically present but semantically weak; ~70% of claims carry markers. Diversity: only one explicit 'What [community] would tell you' section (long-form-references); missing distinct community voices (employment-law, negotiation, equity-comp). Single-community framing is the main weakness in an otherwise excellent response.

Non-obviousness is high: the PTEP-as-cash-deadline reframe, AMT crystallizing before liquidity, forfeiture-on-competitor clauses voiding the cliff, and the adverse-selection read on the Series B's timing inflexibility are all genuine blind spots beyond the obvious 'just wait.' No 'What [community] would tell you' sections present — the response uses 'What long-form-references would tell you' (singular reference voice) rather than distinct community analyst sections, so diversity_count is 0.
